description_long: This house features a classic timber-framed design with beige plaster infill and dark wooden beams, reminiscent of traditional Tudor architecture. The steeply pitched gable roof is covered in reddish-brown shingles, and a prominent stone chimney rises along one side. The front facade includes a small covered porch with wooden steps, a central wooden door with a cross motif, and decorative timber framing. Windows are small and gridded, with the rear and side elevations showing symmetrical arrangements. The base of the house is constructed from gray stone blocks, and there are bushes and greenery at the foundation, adding a touch of landscaping. The overall structure sits on a green plot, suggesting a yard or garden area.
description: A timber-framed, Tudor-style house with a steep gable roof, stone chimney, and decorative wooden beams, set on a landscaped green base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a wooden door, covered porch, steps, and decorative timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the tall stone chimney and visible greenery at the base, with minimal window detail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying symmetrical gridded windows, extensive timber framing, and a solid stone foundation.
description_view_3: Back view highlighting a central window, overhanging upper story, and the continuation of the gable roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with small gridded panes
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with a cross motif, covered porch, wooden steps, and decorative timber framing
